data.four_set_effect: Increases damage caused by Overloaded, Electro-Charged and Superconduct by 40%. Triggering such effects decreases Elemental Skill CD by 1s. Can only occur once every 0.8s.

circlet_of_logos.desc: A crown once worn by an ancient shaman who worshiped the Thunderbird. The capricious beast remained unmoved by the shaman's devotion.
circlet_of_logos.title: Thunder Summoner's Crown

flower_of_life.desc: A lightning-infused flower, somehow spared the fate of being trodden underfoot or reduced to ash by the furious purple fire, making it the sole survivor on the day of disaster.
flower_of_life.title: Thunderbird's Mercy

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: A ceremonial cup that holds the blood of the innocent. It is brimming with the thundering fury of the prayers that echo within.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Omen of Thunderstorm

plume_of_death.desc: A lightning-charged feather that still flickers with the wrath of the Thunderbird's cruel retribution.
plume_of_death.title: Survivor of Catastrophe

sands_of_eon.desc: The hourglass used to foretell the coming of the Thunderbird by the tribe that worshiped it. It has fallen into eternal silence now that the tribe is no more.
sands_of_eon.title: Hourglass of Thunder

list.desc: A purple wild flower that survived in the volcanic ash.</br>It witnessed the total decimation of the ancient tribe.</br>With an innocent's blood, the shaman summoned the Thunderbird.</br>They made their offerings, waiting for the Thunderbird to reveal and the oracles.</br>However, what descended with the Thunderbird, was the lightning of destruction.</br>For the transgression against the young lad who sang to it, the sentence was total obliteration.</br>The Thunderbird showed its true nature, wiping the tribe from the face of the earth.</br>
list.title: Thunderbird's Mercy

list.desc: In the ancient tribe that worshiped the Thunderbird,</br>elder shamans worn this crown.</br>The Thunderbird soared through the thunderstorms, raining down purple thunder and rain.</br>Ancient tribes feared its powers and thanked it for its blessings.</br>Shamans were chosen to offer blood sacrifices to it for protection and forgiveness.</br>The Thunderbird was still a monster that cared not for the worship of mankind.</br>They knew it not, and regarded the unpredictable actions of the Thunderbird as divine revelations.</br>Yet thunder was its breath, as were the fates of men.</br>From the sky, men were no different from animals.</br>Until one day a song pierced through the roaring thunderstorm.</br>The song ripped through the clouds, and bestowed upon it, light.</br>
list.title: Thunder Summoner's Crown

list.desc: A ritual goblet used by the shamans of an ancient tribe.</br>It held the blood to be offered to the Thunderbird.</br>In the seasons when the Thunderbird flew amidst the stormy mountains, a young lad sang without fear.</br>Attracted to his voice, the prideful Thunderbird landed by his side.</br>"Curious tunes, tiny human. Afraid not of thunder and storms?</br>"They said I can bring peace to thunder and storms."</br>The young lad paused and answered.</br>The Thunderbird cried but kept its silence.</br>For his was an enchanting song.</br>It was the first and last meeting between the Thunderbird and the young boy.</br>For the next she saw of the boy was when his blood filled the golden goblet on the sacrificial altar.</br>
list.title: Omen of Thunderstorm

list.desc: The lost feather of the Thunderbird enchanted in purple lightning.</br>It serves as the last witness of the destruction of the tribe.</br>The ancient tribe held the Thunderbird as its protector. But it rained down thunder upon the tribe.</br>On one gloomy night, it shared a pure friendship with the lad.</br>Once it departed, the lad picked up the feather it dropped.</br>"I shall sing you another tune,</br>When you come again with thunder and rain."</br>Driven to madness by the remorse of a promise unkept,</br>The Thunderbird left the mountain in cinders.</br>It was hunted down years after, slain as a monster.</br>Years passed, the burned land revitalized.</br>The lightning-feather was left buried in the vegetation.</br>The story of the Thunderbird and the young lad all but perished with the tribe.</br>
list.title: Survivor of Catastrophe

list.desc: A luxurious hourglass belonging to the ancient tribe that worshiped the Thunderbird.</br>As the tribe faded away, so did the hourglass.</br>A luxurious hourglass, made with amethyst and amber gold, that belonged to a shaman.</br>The hourglass would serve its purpose for the festival held in the Thunderbird's honor.</br>In the last festival the tribe held, the frenzied Thunderbird blew away the bloodstained altar.</br>The countdown to the arrival of the protector became the countdown to the tribe's destruction.</br>The Thunderbird decimated the tribe to avenge the one who sang to it.</br>Unbeknownst to the Thunderbird, the lad himself considered his sacrifice as an offering.</br>All for the good of the tribe, for the blessings of the Thunderbird.</br>
list.title: Hourglass of Thunder
